findings.issue: Wrong EN terminology: 'Shoulder Hacking' is non-standard. Industry term is 'Shoulder Surfing'.
findings.excerpt: surface.en='Shoulder Hacking' — standard EN security term is 'Shoulder Surfing'

findings.issue: EN surface adds 'Remote Access Trojan' expansion but JP source defines RAT as 'Remote Administration Tool / Remote Access Tool'. Concept mismatch between surface.en and definition.jp.
findings.excerpt: surface.en='RAT (Remote Access Trojan)' vs definition.jp='Remote Administration Tool／Remote Access Tool'

findings.issue: ZH surface translates as '远程访问木马（RAT）' (木马=Trojan) but JP definition does not use Trojan framing — calls it Remote Administration/Access Tool. ZH surface mistranslates the concept category.
findings.excerpt: surface.zh='远程访问木马（RAT）' vs JP='Remote Administration Tool／Remote Access Tool'

findings.issue: Section 15-01 '情報セキュリティの脅威' appears as three separate section entities on pages 508, 509, 511 with same section_number. Structural duplication.
Inconsistent EN title for same section 15-01: p508='Threats to Information Security' vs p509/p511='Information Security Threats'.
findings.excerpt: p508: 'Threats to Information Security' | p509/511: 'Information Security Threats'

findings.issue: Safety/security confusion: 'ensuring safety' should be 'ensuring security' in information security context. JP '安全を確保すること' = ensuring security (not physical safety).
findings.excerpt: definition.en: '...ensuring safety against new risks arising from the spread of the Internet'

findings.issue: EN surface 'Trashing' is a transliteration of Japanese term. Standard EN security term is 'Dumpster Diving'. May confuse EN readers.

summary: Audited 36 entities (15 questions, 17 terms, 3 sections, 1 chapter) across 10 pages. Pages 505-506 are intentionally empty (leaf_count=0, stage6_verdict=PASS). Found 5 release-level and 2 warn-level findings. Key defects: (1) safety/security confusion in 情報セキュリティ EN definition ('ensuring safety' should be 'ensuring security'); (2) RAT surface.en/zh adds 'Trojan' framing inconsistent with JP source definition ('Remote Administration Tool'); (3) 'Shoulder Hacking' is non-standard EN — industry term is 'Shoulder Surfing'; (4) Inconsistent EN title for section 15-01 across pages 508/509/511. All 17 terms consistent with glossary. All 15 answer_index values semantically verified correct. No U+FFFD, no hiragana-in-ZH, no duplicate entity IDs, no empty required fields.
